subject: Ecology
title: Anatomy of a Forest
description: One-minute skill: Anatomy of a Forest.

hook.text: A forest is not just trees. It's a layered world stacked from soil to sky.

buildup.text: The canopy captures sunlight. Below it, the understory catches what filters through.

discovery.text: The forest floor teems with decomposers recycling dead leaves back into nutrients.

twist.text: Roots and fungi form underground networks that can span entire mountainsides.

climax.text: Each layer houses different species, creating vertical biodiversity within one stand.

punchline.text: A forest is a skyscraper made of life.

quiz.question: What creates vertical biodiversity in a forest?
options: Distinct layers from floor to canopy
